content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 5, 1994

Carol Engelhardt
965 Avocado Crest Road
La Habra Heights, CA 90631

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-267

Dear Ms. Engelhardt:

This is in response to your letter requesting a confirmation
of telephone advice provided to you by this office regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Please note that nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken
place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented
to us. The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in
providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

May you give your campaign funds to a city?

CONCLUSION

The donation of campaign funds to a city is permissible so
long as (1) the donation is reasonably related to a political
purpose, (2) the recipient is a bona fide charitable, educational,
civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt nonprofit organization,
and (3) no substantial part of the proceeds of the donation will
have a material financial effect on you.

FACTS

You have recently been elected to the La Habra Heights City
Council. You have funds remaining in your campaign account which
you would like to contribute to the city towards its graffiti
removal program.

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-267
Page 2


DISCUSSION

The Act regulates the appropriate use of campaign funds.
(Section 89513 et seq.) Campaign funds may be donated to any bona
fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-
exempt nonprofit organization, where no substantial part of the
proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former
candidate or elected officer, any member of his or her immediate
family, or his or her campaign treasurer.  (Section 89515.) We
have specifically advised that a donation of campaign funds to a
public school is permissible.  (Jasper Advice Letter, No. A-90-
511.)  Similarly, a donation of surplus campaign funds to a city
is permissible as well.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:  Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

SGC/JET/jt
